content: GLP-1 (glucagon-like peptide-1) receptor agonists are incretin mimetics that enhance glucose-dependent insulin secretion, suppress glucagon, slow gastric emptying, and promote satiety. Endogenous GLP-1 secreted by L-cells in response to food intake; rapidly degraded by DPP-4 enzyme (half-life <2 minutes). GLP-1 RAs resistant to DPP-4 degradation. Available agents: short-acting (exenatide BID, lixisenatide) primarily affect postprandial glucose via gastric emptying; long-acting (liraglutide, semaglutide, dulaglutide, exenatide ER) affect fasting and postprandial glucose. Semaglutide available as weekly injection and oral formulation. Mechanism: activate GLP-1 receptors on pancreatic beta cells (insulin secretion), alpha cells (glucagon suppression), brain (satiety centers), GI tract (delayed emptying). Cardiovascular benefits demonstrated in multiple outcome trials.
